From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1969 studio album by Gene Ammons

| The Boss Is Back! | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| Released | 1969 |
| Recorded | November 10 & 11, 1969 |
| Studio | [Van Gelder Studio](/wiki/Van_Gelder_Studio "Van Gelder Studio"), Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Length | 39:40 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") PR 7739 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Bob Porter](/wiki/Bob_Porter_(record_producer) "Bob Porter (record producer)") |

| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[Bad! Bossa Nova](/wiki/Bad!_Bossa_Nova "Bad! Bossa Nova")* (1962) | ***The Boss Is Back!*** (1969) | *[Brother Jug!](/wiki/Brother_Jug! "Brother Jug!")* (1969) | | |

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") | StarStarStarStar[[1]](#cite_note-Allmusic-1) |
| *[The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stone_Jazz_Record_Guide "The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide")* | StarStarStar[[2]](#cite_note-RSJRG-2) |
| *[The Penguin Guide to Jazz Recordings](/wiki/The_Penguin_Guide_to_Jazz_Recordings "The Penguin Guide to Jazz Recordings")* | StarStarHalf star[[3]](#cite_note-Penguin-3) |

***The Boss Is Back!*** is an album by saxophonist [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") recorded in 1969 and released on the [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") label.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It was his second charting album, reaching No. 174 on the [*Billboard* Top LPs](/wiki/Billboard_Top_LPs "Billboard Top LPs"), during a two-week run on the chart.[[5]](#cite_note-Whitburn-5)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Boss_Is_Back!&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

[Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") awarded the album 4 stars with its review by [Scott Yanow](/wiki/Scott_Yanow "Scott Yanow") stating, "The executives at Prestige must have been felt ecstatic when they heard Gene Ammons first play after his release from a very severe seven-year jail sentence. The great tenor proved to still be in his prime, his huge sound was unchanged and he was hungry to make new music... Ammons shows that he had not forgotten how to jam the blues either".[[1]](#cite_note-Allmusic-1)

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Boss_Is_Back!&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

All compositions by Gene Ammons except where noted.

1. "The Jungle Boss" (Gene Ammons, Junior Mance) – 5:36
2. "[I Wonder](/wiki/I_Wonder_(1944_song) "I Wonder (1944 song)")" ([Cecil Gant](/wiki/Cecil_Gant "Cecil Gant"), Raymond Leveen) – 7:59
3. "[Feeling Good](/wiki/Feeling_Good "Feeling Good")" ([Leslie Bricusse](/wiki/Leslie_Bricusse "Leslie Bricusse"), [Anthony Newley](/wiki/Anthony_Newley "Anthony Newley")) – 5:42
4. "Tastin' the Jug" – 7:28
5. "[Here's That Rainy Day](/wiki/Here%27s_That_Rainy_Day "Here's That Rainy Day")" ([Johnny Burke](/wiki/Johnny_Burke_(lyricist) "Johnny Burke (lyricist)"), [Jimmy Van Heusen](/wiki/Jimmy_Van_Heusen "Jimmy Van Heusen")) – 6:07
6. "Madame Queen" – 6:48

**Note**

* Recorded at Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey on November 10 (tracks 1, 2 & 4–6) and November 11 (track 3), 1969

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Boss_Is_Back!&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") – [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone")
* Prince James, [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person") – tenor saxophone (track 1)
* [Junior Mance](/wiki/Junior_Mance "Junior Mance") – [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano") (tracks 1, 2 & 4–6)
* [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") [organ](/wiki/Electronic_organ "Electronic organ") (track 3)
* [Buster Williams](/wiki/Buster_Williams "Buster Williams") – [bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass") (tracks 1, 2 & 4–6)
* [Bob Bushnell](/wiki/Bob_Bushnell_(musician) "Bob Bushnell (musician)") – [electric bass](/wiki/Electric_bass "Electric bass") (track 3)
* Frankie Jones (tracks 1, 2 & 4–6), [Bernard Purdie](/wiki/Bernard_Purdie "Bernard Purdie") (track 3) – [drums](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit")
* [Candido](/wiki/Candido_Camero "Candido Camero") – [congas](/wiki/Congas "Congas") (tracks 1, 2 & 4–6)
